display.title: Regional Campaign Manager Central
display.description: Regional Campaign Manager Campaigns Department Full Time Remote in AR, IA, IL, KS, LA, MI, MN, MO, OK, TX, and WI with occasional travel Salary $75,000 to $100,000 ORGANIZATION OVERVIEW Greenlight America is the leading grassroots campaign force powering the nation's clean energy transition at the local level. Founded in 2023, we support local organizations and volunteers working to get clean energy projects built in their communities. In the past two years we have advanced roughly 15 gigawatts of clean power to the grid, approximately 10x the size of the Hoover Dam. POSITION OVERVIEW The Regional Campaign Manager leads Greenlight America's campaigns for clean energy projects across their assigned region. They develop campaign strategies, support partner organizations, cultivate the leadership of volunteers, and track deliverables to ensure that Greenlight America effectively mobilizes grassroots support for clean energy projects. A successful candidate for the position will be a multi-disciplinary campaigner with a blend of skill sets in strategic planning, organizing, communications, lobbying, and coalition management. They will also have a willingness to continually learn new things and approach challenges with a flexible and creative mindset. The position is remote in the assigned region and reports to the Senior Manager, Campaigns. PRIMARY ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES Lead campaigns for clean energy projects in the assigned region. Work with partner organizations and local leaders to mobilize volunteers and win siting approvals for clean energy projects, primarily at the county and township level. Develop campaign plans, including goals, strategies, and tactical timelines, in collaboration with partners. Track campaign milestones and seize opportunities. Provide support to partner organizations and volunteer leaders. Conduct regular check-in calls, host training sessions, and create advocacy materials. Work with other campaign staff on shared resources for Greenlight America's campaign hub model. Build relationships with organizations in the assigned region, including but not limited to environmental, labor, faith, farming, social justice, and business groups, in collaboration with Greenlight America's partnerships team. Oversee grant agreements with partner organizations and ensure deliverables are met. Engage in regular reporting of campaign data. Work with consultants and manage campaign budgets. May occasionally take on campaigns outside of the assigned region. EXPERIENCE AND QUALIFICATIONS We are looking for candidates who have the following qualifications: 3+ years of relevant experience. Strong candidates for this position may have a background in grassroots organizing, issue advocacy, community engagement, political campaigns, coalition management, or similar. Successful track record of achieving ambitious campaign outcomes. Experience at the local level is a strong plus. Strong project management skills and attention to detail. Experience working with partner organizations and volunteers. Willingness to meet people where they are and build alliances across the political spectrum. Excellent communications skills, both verbal and written, and comfort talking to people at all levels of expertise and seniority. Experience with digital campaigning tools and tactics. Willingness to think creatively, take risks, objectively evaluate results, and iterate in a fast-paced environment. Passion for accelerating the clean energy transition. COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS The base annual salary range for this position is $75,000-$100,000. We offer an excellent benefits package which includes 100% employer paid premiums for medical, dental, and vision insurance, unlimited paid time off, monthly technology stipend, and retirement contributions through 401k and Roth plans. TO APPLY Submit a resume and respond to the applicant questionnaire via this link . ​ Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis until the role is filled. If you require accommodations so that you may participate in the selection process, please email accommodations(@)greenlightamerica.org . ​ Due to the volume of applicants, we will only contact applicants whose skill sets, and experience meet our needs. Apply Now Apply Now Regional Campaign Manager Campaigns Department Remote in AR, IA, IL, KS, LA, MI, MN, MO, OK, TX, and WI with occasional travel Full Time $75,000 to $100,000 Apply Now Diversity and Equity Opportunity Greenlight America is proud to be an equal opportunity employer, and an anti-racist organization that prohibits discrimination in its governance, programs and activities on the basis of race, color, national origin, age, disability, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, genetic information, reprisal, marital status, amnesty, status as a covered veteran, because all or part of an individual's income is derived from public assistance or for any other non-merit based factor. Women, people of color, LGBTQ+ people, and members of other historically and presently disenfranchised populations are strongly encouraged to apply. Our goal is for a work experience that has meaning and impact on the world and on our professional lives, and a workplace where you can feel supported and proud. You can learn more about us at greenlightamerica.org . ​ This position also supports Greenlight Action, an affiliated 501(c)(4) organization. While motivated by the same core values, Greenlight Action and Greenlight America operate independently of one another and each organization has its own board of directors. bottom of page

display.title: People Operations Manager
display.description: People Operations Manager Operations Full Time Remote / Occasional Travel Salary $75,000 - $100,000 ORGANIZATION OVERVIEW Greenlight America is a new organization that mobilizes grassroots power to win local siting and permitting campaigns for clean energy projects. We do this by partnering with local groups and activists to run strategic campaigns that win approval for clean energy projects in their communities. Our founding team has led some of the largest grassroots organizing networks in the country, and we are building a scalable model for engaging and mobilizing local residents in support of clean energy. POSITION OVERVIEW The People Operations Manager will play a crucial role managing and running the systems that power our employee experience. This role is responsible for day-to-day HR and operational tasks, ensuring smooth execution of HR processes and supporting staff across the employee lifecycle. ​ A successful candidate for this position will have excellent problem-solving skills, the ability to think strategically, and a hands-on approach to managing day-to-day human resources. They will be adept at juggling multiple priorities and will possess a strong sense of initiative. The ideal candidate will have experience working in a nonprofit setting, a deep commitment to the mission of accelerating the clean energy transition, and a track record of successfully supporting organizational growth and development. They should be highly organized, detail-oriented, and comfortable working both independently and as part of a team. ​ The position is remote and reports to the Senior People Operations Manager. PRIMARY ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES People Operations Serve as a trusted advisor to staff on HR matters, including benefits, policies, and employee questions. Continuously enhance HR systems and administrative processes to improve efficiency and coordination between functions, such as HRIS optimization, workflow enhancements, and data cleanup. Ensure accuracy of HRIS system(s) and maintain up-to-date employee records and documentation. Maintain HR documentation, employee handbooks, and SOP (standard operating procedures) guides. Support benefits administration with our PEO and ensure compliance with federal and multi-state employment regulations. Partner with the Senior People Operations Manager to execute the performance review cycle, including scheduling, tracking, and reporting on completion and outcomes. Support the Senior People Operations Manager in executing compensation processes, including tracking merit increases and promotions. Assist in administering payroll-related data for accuracy. Track PTO and leave requests. Maintain knowledge of emerging trends, best practices, and regulatory updates in human resources to ensure compliance and continuous improvement. Support the Senior People Operations Manager on special projects as needed. ​ ​ Recruiting Talent Acquisition Manage full-cycle hiring logistics in partnership with hiring managers. Lead onboarding and offboarding processes to ensure smooth transitions. ​ ​ Training Development Develop and manage an internship program. Track and ensure completion of HR training and professional development activities. Support with scheduling and administering staff training programs. Support in designing and delivering staff training logistics, materials, and reporting. Collect feedback on training and provide reporting to the Senior People Operations Manager. Culture Engagement Support execution of staff retreats, engagement events, and recognition programs. Support employee engagement survey administration, including reminders, collection, and basic reporting. Collaborate with managers and the Senior People Operations Manager to identify opportunities to strengthen culture and engagement. EXPERIENCE AND QUALIFICATIONS We are especially interested in candidates with the following qualifications: ​ 3+ years experience of relevant professional experience in human resources or people operations Experience with multi-state employment compliance Ability to analyze and resolve complex issues Ability to manage multiple tasks simultaneously Passion for accelerating the clean energy transition Preferred: experience in California human resources practices COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS The base annual salary for this position is $75,000-$100,000. We offer an excellent benefits package which includes 100% employer paid premiums for medical, dental, and vision insurance, unlimited paid time off, monthly technology stipend, and retirement contributions through 401k and Roth plans. TO APPLY Please apply at this link by submitting a resume and cover letter. Priority applicants will be considered through April 17, 2026. If you require accommodations so that you may participate in the selection process, please email accommodations (@) greenlightamerica.org . ​ Due to the volume of applicants, we will only contact applicants whose skill sets, and experience meet our needs. Apply Now People Operations Manager Operations Remote / Occasional Travel Full Time $75,000 - $100,000 Apply Now Diversity and Equity Opportunity Greenlight America is proud to be an equal opportunity employer, and an anti-racist organization that prohibits discrimination in its governance, programs and activities on the basis of race, color, national origin, age, disability, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, genetic information, reprisal, marital status, amnesty, status as a covered veteran, because all or part of an individual's income is derived from public assistance or for any other non-merit based factor. Women, people of color, LGBTQ+ people, and members of other historically and presently disenfranchised populations are strongly encouraged to apply. Our goal is for a work experience that has meaning and impact on the world and on our professional lives, and a workplace where you can feel supported and proud. You can learn more about us at greenlightamerica.org . This position also supports Greenlight Action, an affiliated 501(c)(4) organization. While motivated by the same core values, Greenlight America and Greenlight Action operate independently of one another and each organization has its own board of directors. bottom of page
